description: Simple required-field validation on Account — exercises the positive + negative_validation coverage flow.

requirement.jira_description: Sales requested that a new Account with Type='Customer' cannot be saved unless Industry is populated. This is enforced via a validation rule.
requirement.acceptance_criteria:
Given a new Account
When Type='Customer' and Industry is empty
Then save is rejected with the validation message 'Industry is required for customer accounts.'

Given a new Account
When Type='Prospect'
Then Industry can remain empty and the save succeeds.

validation_rules: Account.Industry_Required_For_Customer: Industry must be populated when Type='Customer'

expected.__comment__: For DRY-MODE runs, supply an `output` dict that the scorer can evaluate. This lets us exercise the harness without calling the model. In LIVE mode, the scorer uses the model's actual output instead.

test_plan.explanation: Mix of happy-path (Type=Prospect, no Industry required) plus two negative-validation checks for Type=Customer with / without Industry.

test_cases.title: Positive: Prospect without Industry saves cleanly
test_cases.description: Account with Type='Prospect' + no Industry should save.

test_cases.title: Negative: Customer without Industry fails validation
test_cases.description: Type='Customer' + empty Industry should be rejected by VR.
expected_results: Create is rejected with validation message 'Industry is required for customer accounts.'
